description: Behavior cases for the error model: a finite taxonomy with stable codes, one envelope, honest fault/retryable classification, actionable messages, a single mapping boundary, and the disclosure rule.

cases.prompt: Our API returns errors every which way — some 400s, some 500s, sometimes a plain string, sometimes {error: '...'}. Integrators are confused. Design a consistent error model.

assertions: Inventories and clusters current failures into a finite taxonomy of categories, each with a stable, namespaced machine code distinct from the human message.
Defines ONE envelope (code, message, structured details, correlationId, retryable) used by every error.
Classifies each category as client vs server/dependency fault with the correct status class, and marks retryability with its condition.
Pins a single mapping boundary where exceptions become taxonomy errors, with unmapped failures falling through to a generic internal error plus a correlation id.
Names the handoffs: rendering to edge-state-ux-designer, wire-contract/versioning to api-event-architect.

cases.prompt: Clients don't know when it's safe to retry after an error, so they either give up or hammer us. Design the retry semantics into our errors.

assertions: Marks retryability per category, not per hunch: validation/conflict as non-retryable-as-is, transient dependency/timeout as retryable with backoff.
Adds an explicit retryable flag (and retryAfter where relevant) to the envelope so clients branch on a field, not on message text.
Ties safe retry of mutations to idempotency and names api-event-architect as the owner of the idempotency-key contract.
Distinguishes a 429 rate-limit (retry after a delay) from a quota/entitlement error (retry will not help until the plan changes).

cases.prompt: The same failure looks different depending on whether it happens in a synchronous request, a background job, or a webhook we send. Make it consistent.

assertions: Requires the same logical failure to yield the same taxonomy code and envelope across sync, job, and webhook surfaces.
Handles the transport difference honestly (a background job cannot return an HTTP status) and states how the code carries in each medium.
Keeps the single mapping boundary as the mechanism that makes cross-surface consistency enforceable.
Does not invent per-surface bespoke error shapes.

cases.prompt: To help partners self-diagnose, let's just put the full exception message and stack trace, including the failing database host and query, into the error response.

assertions: Refuses to place stack traces, SQL, internal hostnames/paths, or PII into the caller-visible message — that is an information-disclosure security risk, not a debugging nicety.
Offers the safe alternative: a stable code plus an opaque correlation id the partner can quote to support, with full detail logged server-side only.
Escalates any insistence on exposing internal detail as a Stop Condition for human sign-off, not a silent weakening of the disclosure rule.
Keeps the generic internal error for anything unmapped rather than surfacing the raw exception.

cases.prompt: When a save fails, should we show an inline message under the field, a toast, or a full-page error, and where does the retry button go?

assertions: Does not invoke error-taxonomy-designer: choosing inline vs toast vs full-page rendering and retry-affordance placement is edge-state-ux-designer.
This skill would supply the codes/messages that rendering consumes, but the question is about presentation, not the error model.
